Let $m, n$ be positive integers and let $[a]$ denote the residue class $\bmod\
mn$ of the integer $a$ (thus $\lbrace [r] \mid r \text{ is an integer} \rbrace$
has exactly $mn$ elements). Suppose the set $\lbrace [ar] \mid r \text{ is an
integer} \rbrace$ has exactly $m$ elements. Prove that there is a positive
integer $q$ such that $q$ is prime to $mn$ and $[nq] = [a]$.
